creator: ceri@submonkey.net
text: On Fri, Nov 03, 2006 at 09:01:11AM +0100, Rudolf Cejka wrote: > Ceri Davies wrote (2006/11/02): > > > I think that I have found a solution here - the patch below simply > > > joins two ... blocks into one, where white space removal > > > does not occur in sgml nor in xsl case. > > It's wrong though; they are separate span's and should be marked up as > > such. > > Why? I think that ... just wants to > say, when to display the text included. And if there should be skipped > or included two adjacent texts, why the space between then should be > displayed in all cases? (The space is then removed probably too, but > I wanted to express logical explanation here, why I do not understand, > why there should be two spans and not just one.) Yeah, you're probably right. Ceri -- That must be wonderful! I don't understand it at all. -- Moliere
